sections.content:
OK I see the pattern now. The cursor alternates between the sky blue bar and the pink bar when I press UP/DOWN. When the cursor is on the pink bar, the second symbol in the pink bar changes. Let me understand the game mechanics. The cursor highlights a symbol, and when it moves away, the symbol reverts to its original state. This is a toggle mechanism. When the cursor is on the pink bar, the second symbol changes. When it moves away, the symbol reverts.
Now I need to understand the goal. The upper grid has 12 cells. The lower panel has 10 symbols. The goal is to match the symbols in the upper grid with the symbols in the lower panel. The cursor can move between the two bars. When the cursor is on the pink bar, the second symbol changes. When it moves away, the symbol reverts.
Wait, but the second symbol in the pink bar changes when the cursor is on the pink bar. Let me look at the image, the second symbol in the pink bar is a plus sign. When the cursor is on the pink bar, the second symbol changes to a different symbol. When it moves away, the symbol reverts.
Actually, I think the cursor is selecting a symbol from the lower panel to match a symbol from the upper grid. The goal is to select the correct symbol from the lower panel to match the upper grid.
Let me try pressing RIGHT to move the cursor right, and see if it moves to the next symbol.

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
